extras.definition: U.S. electronic network for batch credit and debit transfers between bank accounts, governed by NACHA rules.
Share of enrolled loyalty or subscription members who transacted in the last 90 days.
HL7 messaging family carrying patient movement events; backbone of EHR-to-downstream integration.
Share of card authorisation attempts that are declined for any reason.
Annualised value of active subscriptions on a normalised basis, excluding one-time fees.
Hierarchical list of components, sub-assemblies, and quantities needed to build or service a finished good.
Customer Average Interruption Duration Index — minutes off per interruption experienced.
Quality system process for investigating, correcting, and preventing recurrence of defects or non-conformances.
One minus the mean absolute percentage error of treasury cash forecasts versus actuals.
U.S. Federal Reserve supervisory exercise assessing capital adequacy of large bank holding companies.
Card disputes raised by issuers as a share of total card sales over a window.
Discounted future revenue (or margin) attributable to a customer over the relationship.
Group of patients, customers, or assets sharing a defined characteristic and tracked over time.
Ad price per thousand impressions; benchmark for digital and linear inventory monetisation.
Share of scheduled flights operated on the day of departure, net of cancellations.
Defective units detected per million received from a supplier — quality scorecard input.
Share of submitted healthcare claims rejected by payers on first adjudication.
Share of hotel bookings transacted on the brand's own channels rather than via OTAs or GDS.
Average days between invoice issuance and cash collection.
System of record for clinical patient data — orders, notes, results, encounters.
Federally mandated device that records driver hours-of-service for commercial fleets.
Reporting framework covering environmental impact, social performance, and governance practices.
Operating cash flow less capex; primary measure of corporate liquidity generation.
HL7 standard for exchanging healthcare information using RESTful resources.
Units passing all quality checks on the first attempt as a share of units started.
Total value of goods sold through a marketplace before take rate, returns, or discounts.
NCQA quality-of-care measure set widely used by U.S. health plans.
aliases: Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set

extras.definition: U.S. DOT/FMCSA rules limiting driving hours for commercial motor vehicle operators.
Airline schedule disruptions including cancellations, delays, and diversions.
Global XML/JSON message standard for financial communications, replacing legacy SWIFT MT.
Cash held in non-yielding accounts above operating buffer — opportunity-cost target for treasury.
Fee paid by merchants' acquirers to card issuers for processing card transactions.
Customer identification, verification, and risk-rating program required of regulated financial institutions.
HQLA divided by 30-day net stressed cash outflows; Basel III liquidity standard.
Combined work-in-process plus AR days — key cash-tied-up metric in professional services.
4-digit ISO classification of merchants by business type, used for routing and pricing.
Discipline and platform for governing canonical reference and master entities across systems.
Mortgage Industry Standards Maintenance Organization XML formats used in origination and servicing.
Available stable funding divided by required stable funding; Basel III long-term liquidity rule.
Availability multiplied by performance multiplied by quality — composite manufacturing productivity score.
Standardised analytics-ready schema for observational healthcare data used in RWE.
aliases: Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership Common Data Model

extras.definition: Customer orders shipped or received by the committed date as a share of total orders.
Third-party travel reseller (e.g. Expedia, Booking.com) that distributes hotel and air inventory.
95th-percentile end-to-end response time; common SLO benchmark for APIs and pipelines.
Share of tested die that pass parametric (electrical performance) tests.
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard governing handling of cardholder data.
Health information that identifies an individual; subject to HIPAA Privacy & Security Rules.
CMS-derived score reflecting expected medical cost based on member demographics and HCCs.
Mismatch between two records (e.g., trade vs custody) that requires investigation and adjustment.
Hours between receipt of a return and credit issuance to the customer.
Share of subscription contracts due for renewal that successfully renew.
Hotel performance metric — room revenue divided by total available room nights.
System Average Interruption Duration Index — outage minutes per customer served.
System Average Interruption Frequency Index — interruptions per customer served.
Regulatory filing made by a financial institution to report potentially illicit activity.
Share of planned production volume actually produced on the planned line and shift.
Direct (owned) plus indirect (purchased energy) greenhouse gas emissions for a reporting entity.
Dimensional modeling pattern that preserves history of attribute changes by adding rows with effective dates.
Average elapsed time between transaction authorisation and final settlement to the merchant.
CMS aggregate rating (1–5) of Medicare Advantage plan quality and member experience.
Share of transactions processed end-to-end without manual intervention.
Dynamic uplift on base price applied to balance demand and supply in real time.
Marketplace revenue divided by GMV — the platform's share of transaction value.
Share of cloud resources carrying mandatory cost-allocation and ownership tags.
Share of devices or users actively allowing collection of usage and reliability telemetry.
Days from case filing to final court disposition.
200,000 multiplied by recordable incidents divided by hours worked — OSHA safety benchmark.
Periodic reconciliation of consumed versus contracted units, often resulting in additional billing.
Approve, decline, or counter-offer outcome on a loan or insurance application.
Per-unit (customer, ride, transaction) revenue and cost analysis used to assess scaling viability.
Billable hours divided by available hours for consultants and engineers.
Average traffic carried as a share of capacity on a telecom transport or RAN element.
IAB standard XML format for delivering video ads to player clients.
Calendar days from wafer start to completion across the fab process flow.
Share of churned customers reactivated within a specified window.
Inventory of goods that have entered production but not yet been completed.
XML-based standard for tagging business and financial data; mandated for many regulatory filings.
Acute inpatient discharge followed by another inpatient admission within 30 days.
Loan or card balance more than 30 days past its scheduled payment date.
Authentication protocol layered onto card transactions to shift liability for fraud.
HIPAA standard format for electronic submission of healthcare claims to payers.
Bank or processor that contracts with merchants to accept card payments.
